tools.name: OCR
tools.description: This tool can recognize all text on the input image.

outputs.description: OCR results, include bbox in x1, y1, x2, y2 format and the recognized text.

tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: You are given a histology image. Determine which organ is shown.

dialogs.thought: Check for any labels or scale text that might indicate organ or stain.
Orient to tissue architecture and key histologic patterns.

content.content: Grayscale histology micrograph showing plates/cords of polygonal cells with intervening sinusoid-like spaces. A central pale fibrous area contains a round vascular lumen and adjacent smaller duct-like profiles, suggestive of a portal tract; surrounding parenchyma has lobular architecture.

arguments.attribute: histologic structures present (e.g., portal triad, central vein, hepatocyte cords, sinusoids)

dialogs.thought: Verify presence of portal tract/triad and hepatocyte-sinusoid pattern to support liver identification.

content.content: Within this region there is a pale connective tissue focus containing a larger thin-walled vascular lumen with nearby smaller round duct-like structures; surrounding tissue shows cords of polygonal cells separated by narrow sinusoidal channels, consistent with a portal tract in hepatic parenchyma.

dialogs.content: The image shows a section of the liver.
